title: Bytes, Unicode, Newlines, Front Matter, Metadata, and Schemas · Behavior Case: Explicit Decoding
summary: The build relies on locale encoding, silently replaces invalid bytes, parses arbitrary YAML objects, accepts duplicate metadata keys, and compares dates in mixed formats. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the explicit decoding increment in versioned decoded-document and metadata contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, cha…
objectives: Decode source bytes with declared UTF-8 and error policy while preserving path, byte count, newline behavior, and causal diagnostics.
Record stable source identity, relative path, size, digest purpose, route candidate, status, and rejection reason.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: ssg-explicit-decoding — Decode source bytes with declared UTF-8 and error policy while preserving path, byte count, newline behavior, and causal diagnostics.
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-text-encoding-metadata-5
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-text-encoding-metadata-5.
# Case 9036: an offline-first field documentation build.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks.
def worked_static_site_text_encoding_metadata_5(byte_count: int = 240, decoded_units: int = 228, metadata_fields: int = 6, decode_errors: int = 0, schema_errors: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "readerworked5",
        "source9036",
        "parse5dekrow",
        "routeworked59036",
        "artifact9036worked5",
        "recovery5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if byte_count < 0 or decoded_units < 0:
        return False, decoded_units, "text-count"
    if decoded_units > byte_count:
        return False, decoded_units, "encoding-units"
    if metadata_fields < 3:
        return False, metadata_fields, "metadata-shape"
    if decode_errors > 0 or schema_errors > 0:
        return False, decode_errors + schema_errors, "invalid-document"
    assert byte_count >= decoded_units
    return True, metadata_fields, "validated-document"

# Evidence: ssg-explicit-decoding-1dmoya
# Competency: ssg-explicit-decoding.
# Case 9766: a multilingual public-service handbook migration.
# Operating constraint: reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval.

def evidence_ssg_explicit_decoding_1dmoya(byte_count: int = 240, decoded_units: int = 228, metadata_fields: int = 6, decode_errors: int = 0, schema_errors: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1dmoya = "1dmoya-ayomd1-9766"
    assert evidence_variant_1dmoya
    evidence_axes_1dmoya = (
        "reader1dmoya",
        "source9766",
        "parseayomd1",
        "route1dmoya9766",
        "artifact97661dmoya",
        "recoveryayomd19766",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1dmoya)) == 6
    if byte_count < 0 or decoded_units < 0:
        return False, decoded_units, "text-count"
    if decoded_units > byte_count:
        return False, decoded_units, "encoding-units"
    if metadata_fields < 3:
        return False, metadata_fields, "metadata-shape"
    if decode_errors > 0 or schema_errors > 0:
        return False, decode_errors + schema_errors, "invalid-document"
    assert byte_count >= decoded_units
    return True, metadata_fields, "validated-document"

steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Explicit Decoding: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 9950 during a large technical reference incremental rebuild. Build deterministic Python evidence to decode UTF-8 content and validate bounded front matter without hiding data loss; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Decode source bytes with declared UTF-8 and error policy while preserving path, byte count, newline behavior, and causal diagnostics.
